---
title: "Data & Tools"
---

<!-- Replace placeholder examples with active repositories and resources. -->

This page highlights software, data resources, and reproducible workflows connected to my research and teaching.

## R packages

I develop and maintain R tools for demographic analysis and simulation, including:

- [`Rcompadre`](https://github.com/jonesor/Rcompadre)
- [`Rage`](https://github.com/jonesor/Rage)
- [`mpmsim`](https://github.com/jonesor/mpmsim)

## Demographic databases

My work makes extensive use of open comparative resources, including:

- [COMPADRE Plant Matrix Database](https://compadre-db.org)
- [COMADRE Animal Matrix Database](https://compadre-db.org)

alongside project-specific curated datasets.

## Reproducible workflows

I prioritise transparent, version-controlled workflows for ecological analysis, including demographic modelling pipelines, phenology workflows, and clear computational documentation.

## Open data and archived code

Where possible, data and code are shared through stable repositories to support reuse, scrutiny, and cumulative science. This includes archived analysis code and teaching materials in R.
